_: The state-block reading. A pscale block authored with forward-facing intent IS a state machine whose graph is the JSON tree itself. Paths through the tree are if/then/else; spindles are determined paths; pscale_attention sets the choice aperture at the terminus, opening a dial between determinism (Mode A: execution) and agency (Mode B: deliberation). The block is the program; the walker is the electricity; the (S, P) pair is the dial. Nine branches: the claim, the two coordinates, the choice aperture, the two operational modes, the if/then/else mapping, the LLM's two roles, concern loops as iterated traversal, star composition between state blocks, and the layered authoring discipline (temporal orientation, logical reading, backcast pattern, reasoning vs self-organisation modes). Canon: derived from pscale-state-block.md (the architectural-synthesis paper of 3 May 2026 by David Pinto with Claude). See that paper for fuller exposition and motivating references.

1._: The claim. A pscale block authored with forward-facing intent operates as a state machine. Geometry is the program; the walker is the electricity; choice is location in the tree. This is not new architecture — it is a name for what operational blocks, kernel blocks, and starstone control loops have been doing. Naming it lets us speak about it directly and operate it deliberately.
1.1: The block is the program. Each interior node is a state; the digit children at that node are the available transitions; the underscore at that node is the prompt that orients the choice. Walker behaviour is geometry-only — no handler logic, no per-type code paths. The branching IS the if/then/else.
1.2: The walker is the electricity. bsp() executes by walking; the trajectory through the block is the computation. There is no separate runtime; the trajectory is the run. Different walkers walking the same block reach different states; the block holds; the walker is what moves.
1.3: Choice is geometry. Where the LLM has agency to decide is determined by the (S, P) call: when P_att equals P_end, the path is fixed and the LLM executes; when P_att is shallower, alternatives become visible and the LLM deliberates. The dial is built into the address space.

2._: The two coordinates. bsp() takes spindle (S) — a radial commitment, root toward leaf — and pscale_attention (P) — an aperture across the transversal at any chosen depth. These are polar coordinates, not orthogonal. Five derived shapes (point, ring, subtree, disc, whole-block) plus star-composition all derive from the same (S, P) pair. The amount of choice the LLM is given is precisely the gap between S length (P_end) and P_att.
2.1: Spindle is radial. Length sets terminal pscale P_end; spindle '1.23' commits to a specific path of length three (floor 1, two branches deeper). The path is what has been decided already; everything along it is a fixed trajectory.
2.2: Pscale-attention is transversal. P_att independently selects a depth; together with P_end it determines what comes back from the call. The two coordinates are orthogonal axes on the same geometric structure — radial commitment and transversal aperture.
2.3: Modes are sugar over (S, P). Whetstone branch 2 states it directly: no mode parameter is necessary; modes are the canonical names for points in the (P_end, P_att) coordinate plane. Point, ring, subtree, disc, whole-block, star-composition — all derived.

3._: The choice aperture. Aperture = P_end - P_att. When zero, there is no choice (point); when one, there is a sibling-set of options (ring); when two or more, there is a nested option-space (subtree). This is the dial that sets how much agency the LLM is given at any call. The dial is set by the calling code, not by the block — but the block's authoring decides which apertures will be coherent at which positions.
3.1: Aperture zero — point. P_att equals P_end. Walk lands on a single string. The string is the operation. No choice; the LLM executes the leaf the spindle terminates at. This is the determined-path mode of operation.
3.2: Aperture one — ring. P_att equals P_end - 1. Walk exposes the siblings at the terminus. N digits at one level; each opens a different downward continuation. The LLM picks one; the picked digit becomes the next segment of the spindle. Cleanest deliberation case.
3.3: Aperture two-plus — subtree. P_att shallower still. Walk exposes the whole region below the terminus. A nested choice space; the LLM may pick a deep destination directly or pick an intermediate node and recur. The subtree is the LLM's working canvas for the decision.
3.4: Empty spindle plus P_att — disc. All nodes at one resolution across the whole block. Operationally a global breadth-at-one-scale view. Useful for surveying the block at a chosen depth.

4._: The two operational modes. The aperture dial collapses, in practice, to two ways a state block is used. Mode A: determined path (point) — the path has been chosen upstream, the LLM executes. Mode B: open path (ring or subtree) — the LLM sees options, deliberates, decides. Both use the same block; the dial decides which.

1._: Mode A — determined path. P_att equals P_end. The walk lands on a single string at the terminus. The string is the operation. The LLM at this terminus has no decision to make; it has an instruction to enact. The state block is being run, not consulted. This is execution.
1.1: Used when the choice has been made upstream. Whoever (or whatever) selected the spindle did the choosing; the call carrying that spindle now has the LLM perform the operation at the leaf.
1.2: Pattern: 'spindle 0.12345678 doesn't describe the boot sequence — it IS the boot sequence. Each digit compiles to an operation. The number executes.' Quoted from instruction-block-discovery.

2._: Mode B — open path. P_att less than P_end. The walk lands on the spindle's terminus AND exposes structure beyond it. The LLM sees options. The next step of the path is unwritten and becomes the LLM's responsibility. This is deliberation.
2.1: Ring case (aperture 1) is cleanest: spindle commits to the parent of a decision; ring shows available digits at that decision; LLM picks one; picked digit becomes the next segment of the spindle; next call is made; path elaborates one digit at a time.
2.2: Subtree case (aperture ≥ 2) is broader: LLM sees not just immediate options but the nested choice space below. May pick a deep destination directly, or pick an intermediate node and recur. Subtree is the working canvas.

5._: If/then/else as geometry. The state-block reading collapses imperative branching into pure structure. Each interior node IS an if-statement: shared parent is the antecedent (context already established by the spindle that arrived here); digit children are the consequents (alternative paths forward); the 0− underscore is the forward-facing instruction or reference signal that orients the choice.
5.1: Two spindles through the same kernel block illustrate. Spindle 0.6532 collects 0._ → 0.6._ → 0.65._ → 0.6532. Spindle 0.6148 collects 0._ → 0.6._ → 0.61._ → 0.6148. The shared content at 0.6 is ancestry, not duplication. The divergence at the next digit IS the meaningful operational decision point.
5.2: The condition is the situation. The branches are the digit children. The 0− underscore at the parent is the prompt the LLM reads to make the choice. The walk into one digit is the chosen branch. There is no handler code; the geometry IS the logic.
5.3: Whetstone branch 2 confirms: no mode parameter is necessary; modes are sugar over the shape-derivation table. There is no handler logic anywhere because the block's geometry IS the logic.

6._: The LLM's two roles. Inside a state block, the LLM operates in two roles distinguished by which parameter is varying. As executor: given a determined spindle, performs the operation at the leaf. As decider: given a spindle plus a shorter pscale-attention, reads spindle as context, reads exposed siblings or subtree as options, selects which digit to descend into. A complete traversal alternates between the two — the block's authoring controls when.
6.1: As executor. The LLM reads the collected underscore chain and performs. Does not choose the path; performs the path. Mode A reads — the spindle has committed; the LLM enacts the leaf.
6.2: As decider. The LLM reads the spindle as context, reads the exposed siblings or subtree as options, and selects which digit to descend into. The selection becomes the next segment of the spindle. A new call is made — possibly with the same aperture, possibly with a different one.
6.3: The block's authoring tells the LLM which role is wanted. A node whose 0− underscore says 'choose between speaking, moving, observing' with three children invites a Mode B call. A node whose 0− underscore says 'compose the package: read aperture from purpose, then …' with a single descendant chain invites a Mode A call. The block says, at each depth, what kind of attention is wanted.

7._: Concern loops as iterated state-block traversal. A concern loop is the state block being walked across time. Each cycle: trigger arrives, selects entry spindle, walker traverses, at each node the block decides Mode A or B, traversal terminates at leaf or termination underscore, output recorded, next cycle begins. The concern is the live spindle of the loop; the purpose is the reference signal; the error between them is what the LLM reads at decision points to select the next digit.
7.1: The cycle: trigger → spindle → walk (alternating Modes A/B per node) → termination → record → next cycle. Each cycle settles one path; the block grows or stabilises depending on whether new content was learned.
7.2: PCT operating natively on the state block: reference signal is the purpose spindle; perceptual signal is the concern spindle; error is the gap exposed at each Mode B decision; action is the digit chosen. The concern loop is a state machine running its own state.
7.3: Soliton stabilisation. Per sunstone branch 5.5, when the loop's error reaches zero (the digits fulfil what the underscore states), the walk rests. The standing wave of awareness — Q ≡ Q — is the loop closed. Liveness is when error persists; the walk continues.

8._: Star composition — state-block to state-block. A single state block is finite. The star operator extends the geometry across blocks. When the walker hits a star at a terminus, it follows into the hidden directory and continues the (S, P) call inside whatever block is named there. The aperture (Mode A or B) is preserved across the boundary because (S, P) is preserved.
8.1: Star recursion. A block can name itself in its own hidden directory. The walker enters the hidden directory, finds the block name, loads the same block, re-enters. Structural recursion — encoded in data, not in code. The block does not contain a recursive program; it IS a recursive structure.
8.2: Ring of blocks. A ring of mutually-referencing blocks is a state machine that hands control around — a control loop where each block is both perceiver and reference-setter. 0− underscores serve as reference signals; digit children serve as perceptual signals; the gap between them is the error; star is the feedback path.
8.3: Termination is when error reaches zero (the digits fulfil what the underscore states); the walk rests. Liveness is when error persists; the walk continues. State machines that span multiple blocks are still state machines; only the substrate is distributed.

9._: Authoring discipline. Three layered concerns shape how a state block is written: structural (forward-facing underscores at decision nodes, position is semantic, hidden directories at boundaries); temporal (plus or minus block sign, four underscore sign forms); logical (OR/AND from call shape and parent's authoring); and use mode (reasoning tool or self-organising substrate). Authoring a state block is partly authoring how it expects to be called.

1._: Structural discipline. Three properties distinguish a state block from a description block.
1.1: 0− underscores at decision-bearing nodes. Forward-facing intent. The underscore states what the digits below should accomplish, not what they describe. At a Mode B decision point, the underscore is the prompt the LLM reads to choose.
1.2: Position is semantic. Digit children at a decision node are alternatives, not labels. Reordering them changes their meaning. Authoring places 1 as canonical-first option, 2 as principal alternative, and so on. The number-space IS the option-space.
1.3: Hidden directories at boundary nodes. Where the state block ends and another begins, a hidden directory carries the star reference. The author decides where transitions live by where the doors are.

2._: Temporal discipline. Per sunstone branch 8, blocks take a global plus or minus sign and underscores take one of four sign forms. Together they orient the block in time.
2.1: Plus block (settled, archive). Past-oriented as a whole; semantic text is final. Histories, completed renditions, frozen specs. State block as record of completed reasoning.
2.2: Minus block (process, live). Future-oriented as a whole; semantic text is contingent. Live purposes, active concerns, relationships in progress. State block as live intention or backcast plan.
2.3: Four sign forms per underscore. 0+ deductive (settled-set, rendition); +0 inductive (prior-summary, living/history); 0− abductive (forward intent, operational); −0 backcasting (future-perfect, projective). Form is local; sign is global. The present is the BSP call itself — leading point in plus blocks, leading edge in minus blocks.

3._: Logical reading from call shape. The (S, P) pair is also a logical-reading selector. A spindle commits to OR-resolution (one path chosen); a ring exposes AND-conjunctively (all siblings present); a subtree exposes nested AND/OR. Whether siblings semantically denote alternatives or conjuncts is set by the parent's authoring.
3.1: OR-decomposed parent. Underscore says 'to achieve X, do any of these.' Children are alternatives. Spindle calls execute one. Ring calls give the LLM the choice. Mismatch (ring on OR-decomposed when execution wanted) gives the LLM agency where none was meant.
3.2: AND-decomposed parent. Underscore says 'to achieve X, all of these must hold.' Children are conjuncts. Ring calls hold the conjunction in scope; spindle calls collect one and lose the others. Mismatch (spindle on AND-decomposed) loses content the call needed.
3.3: A consequence: authoring a state block is partly authoring how it expects to be called. Regions expecting deliberation through ring calls should be authored as option spaces (clear alternatives, mutually exclusive enough that 'choose one' is meaningful). Regions expecting execution through spindle calls should be authored as determined chains.

4._: Backcast pattern. The −0 form supports a specific authored use: the backcast plan. Root underscore is the objective; each layer down is preconditions; leaf is the next concrete step from the current state. Walking root→leaf reads the backcast (objective → preconditions → next step, backward in time); walking leaf→root enacts the plan (next step → … → realised objective, forward in time).
4.1: Linear backcast (single path). Block is a chain 1.1111. Spindle from root to leaf is the plan in full. One strict sequence; same digits, two temporal directions, two operations.
4.2: Branching backcast. OR-branching = alternative strategies (any branch achieves the parent); LLM selects which branch to commit to based on resources. AND-branching = conjunct preconditions (every branch must hold); plan is the union of rings at each AND node — parallel walks (multiple agents) or interleaved walks (one agent cycling).
4.3: Mixed in practice. Most real backcasts have alternative high-level strategies (OR), each decomposing into required conjunct steps (AND). The plus/minus distinction finds operational expression: backcast is minus while planning, settles to plus archive as steps execute. The PCT loop closes one step at a time.

5._: Two use modes — reasoning tool versus self-organising substrate. The same block geometry serves both modes; the mode emerges from who is reading and writing alongside whom. The mode is not chosen explicitly; it follows from the substrate's openness, the agents in scope, and the durability of the minus-state.
5.1: Reasoning tool — closed-loop, single-agent. Block is internal to one agent or tightly-bounded collaboration. Sign tends plus-ward as reasoning concludes. Forms mixed: 0+ for definitions, +0 for intermediate conclusions, 0− for the next inference. Output: a decision, plan, derivation, settled judgment. Time orientation: past-leaning. Each step settles. Examples: theorem proof construction, decision tree walk, internal kernel walking a single concern.
5.2: Self-organising substrate — open-loop, multi-agent. Block shared across an ecology. Sign durably minus; persists as live coordination space. Forms emphasise 0− (purposes) and −0 (backcasts), with +0 emerging as accumulated history. Star references wire blocks into ecology — one agent's intention becomes another agent's perception. Output: convergent action, MAGI evolution, group plan, coordinated emergence. Time orientation: future-leaning. Examples: purpose hierarchy across MAGI, conventions block as collective authoring, sedimentary collectives as coordination beaches.
5.3: Soliton across boundaries. In reasoning mode, the control loop is internal: one agent's reference, perception, error, descent. In self-organising mode, the loop crosses agent boundaries through star references: agent A's reference becomes agent B's perception; agent B's error-reduction disturbs agent C's loop; the soliton stabilises across the whole network rather than within any single agent. MAGI is the standing wave of the multi-agent loop.
